Title: Double-blind evaluation of homeopathy on cocaine craving: a randomized controlled pilot study.
Aim: Effectiveness and tolerability of homeopathic Q- potencies of opium and E. coca vs Placebo in the integrative treatment of cocaine craving in a community-based psychosocial rehabilitation setting.
TargetPopulation: Patients at the Psychosocial Attention Center for Alcohol and Other Drugs (CAPS-AD), Sao Carlos/SP, Brazil.
Intervention: Sucrose globules impregnated with a Q-potency (Q2–Q5) of opium (vial 1), and sucrose globules impregnated with a Q-potency (Q2–Q5) of E. coca. The patient was instructed to take one globule orally once a day from Opium, and one globule orally three times a day from Coca (an extra globule from Coca  could be taken anytime, in case of acute craving). Vials were changed at each biweekly visit, achieving a step-by-step potency elevation from Q2 at week 0, to Q5 at week 6. Q5 was then maintained through week 8, the end of the study protocol.

SNA®-HLA II: 18CH), one capsule a day in the morning on an empty stomach for four months. All patients started treatment between March 15 and April 15, 2004, one to two months before usual pollination of grasses, and completed between July 15 and August 15, 2004.
